title: Building Great Agent Skills: The Missing Manual
summary: Matt Pocock introduces a "Skill Checklist Framework" for building high-quality AI agent skills. The framework addresses "skill hell" through four components: Trigger (user-invoked vs model-invoked decisions), Structure (organizing steps and references), Steering (using leading words to guide agent behavior), and Pruning (removing obsolete material and no-ops).
writeup: Matt Pocock presents a "Skill Checklist Framework" for building high-quality AI agent skills, aimed at avoiding what he calls "skill hell." The framework breaks into four components: Trigger (deciding between user-invoked and model-invoked skills), Structure (organizing steps and references), Steering (using leading words to guide agent behavior), and Pruning (removing obsolete material and no-ops). The talk situates these practices within agentic loops and context management, treating skill design as a prompt and structure problem. The takeaway is a concrete checklist for diagnosing why a skill triggers, how it is organized, how it directs the agent, and what should be cut.
